facade_description: 158 Augusta Ave presents a row-house facade within the Kensington Market HCD. The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, row typology. The facade reads primarily as mixed in a black painted storefront cladding at ground, dark brown/painted brick upper floor palette, laid in running bond with light grey mortar joints. The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 1, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance. HCD note: The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that has been modified to accommodate a commercial conversion through a one-storey commercial addition. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the
